description:
You are acting as a Site-Reliability Engineer who is double-checking that the most recent uptime log is always easy to reach.

    1.  The following items are already present on the filesystem:
        •  Directory: /home/user/logs  
        •  File: /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-14_13-45-00.log  
        •  File: /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-15_13-45-00.log  

    2.  Your job is to create (or overwrite, if it already exists) **one** symbolic link named  
           /home/user/uptime_latest.log  
        so that it points **exactly** to  
           /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-15_13-45-00.log

    3.  After creating the link, you must write a one-line verification file:
        •  Path: /home/user/uptime_link_check.txt  
        •  Contents: the fully-resolved (absolute) target path of the new link followed by a single newline.
          Example of the required file content (note the trailing newline):
              /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-15_13-45-00.log\n

        To generate this single line you can, for instance, use a command that resolves the symbolic link and redirect its output to the file, but the exact commands are up to you.

    4.  No additional text, banner, or extra whitespace is allowed inside /home/user/uptime_link_check.txt.  
        Automated tests will confirm:
        •  That /home/user/uptime_latest.log is a symbolic link.  
        •  That it resolves to /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-15_13-45-00.log.  
        •  That /home/user/uptime_link_check.txt contains exactly the single absolute path plus a newline and nothing else.

    Perform the necessary terminal actions to satisfy these conditions.
truth:
Initial filesystem state before the agent starts:
        Directory: /home/user/logs  (permissions 755)
        Files:
            /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-14_13-45-00.log  (non-empty, irrelevant content)
            /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-15_13-45-00.log  (non-empty, irrelevant content)

    Desired final state for verification:
        1.  A symbolic link exists at /home/user/uptime_latest.log
            •  It is a symlink (lstat reports S_IFLNK).
            •  readlink -f /home/user/uptime_latest.log returns exactly:
                  /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-15_13-45-00.log

        2.  Verification file /home/user/uptime_link_check.txt exists
            •  It is a regular file (not a symlink).
            •  File content (including trailing newline, no extra whitespace before/after):
                  /home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-15_13-45-00.log\n   (where \n is ASCII 0x0A)

    The automated test sequence:
        a) readlink -f /home/user/uptime_latest.log  --> must equal the string above.
        b) cat -A /home/user/uptime_link_check.txt  --> must output "/home/user/logs/uptime_2023-09-15_13-45-00.log$" (where $ represents line-end).
        c) stat --format=%F /home/user/uptime_latest.log  --> must output "symbolic link".
